trickyParts: The advance endpoint dispatches workers by making internal HTTP calls to POST /workers on the same server, avoiding a risky refactor of the 330-line inline handler into a shared function
GitHub issueId format is ambiguous for repos with hyphens — parseIssueIdParts uses last-numeric-segment convention, but advance handler uses cachedState.source for canonical owner/repo/number
Linear backend mutations are stubs that throw explicitly — Linear mutations require MCP which is only available in agent sessions, not daemon CLI
CI typecheck caught missing LegionInstancePaths fields (legionStateDir, feedbackFile) and RepoManagerDeps fields (rmDir, symlink) in test mocks — fixed in second push
deviations: Plan suggested extracting POST /workers handler into a shared handleWorkerCreate() function — instead used internal HTTP fetch to POST /workers to reuse existing logic without risky refactoring
Plan suggested separate test files for each task — consolidated into two test files (advance.test.ts for endpoint+config tests, mutations.test.ts for backend mutation tests)
Plan suggested Envoy fast path subscription in daemon/index.ts — deferred to health tick fallback only since daemon Envoy subscription patterns are not yet established
openQuestions: Envoy fast-path for auto-progression (daemon subscribing to worker-done events) deferred to health tick fallback — the daemon does not have an established Envoy consumer pattern yet
GitHub transitionIssue uses Projects V2 GraphQL which requires project field/option IDs fetched dynamically — this could be slow for high-volume auto-progression
